{{Quote|Jango's grunts will provide covering fire and keep the extraction point clear.|[[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]]|<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />}}
"'''Jango's grunts'''" was the name given to a group of [[True Mandalorians|True Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] under the [[Commander/Legends|command]] of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] during the [[Battle of Korda Six|mission to Korda Six]] to rescue pinned-down [[Korda Defense Force|Kordan Defense Force]] personnel from hostile territory. A rearguard [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] unit, "Jango's grunts" were named as such by the True [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]] during the pre-mission briefing; Montross was also responsible for assigning the unit to defend the Mandalorians' extraction point, and provide [[Lightfight|covering fire]] for the True Mandalorians' [[Vertigo Company|other]] [[Headhunter Company|divisions]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

Despite intelligence reports denoting minimal resistance to be expected, the native [[Kordan]]s assaulted the True Mandalorian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in force as they made to land. Amidst the chaos, Jango Fett and his grunts made an improvised push toward the location of the True Mandalorians' targets, only to find a trap laid out for them by the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> a renegade Mandalorian splinter-faction in sworn opposition to the True Mandalorians.<ref name="OpenSeasons1">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]]</ref> Fett's unit was able to repel the ambush—killing several [[Death/Legends|Death]] Watch troops using [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlet]]-mounted [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]]—and moved to reinforce their fellow True Mandalorians on the battlefield, but failed to reach their leader, [[Jaster Mereel]], before he was mortally wounded by the Death Watch's own [[Secret Mandalore|leader]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> [[Tor Vizsla]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> As Mereel died, Fett was chosen to succeed him as [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] of the Mandalorians, and "Jango's grunts" dispersed back into the larger ranks of the True Mandalorian forces.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

==Behind the scenes==
"Jango's grunts" were first introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' in the [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|second issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[W. Haden Blackman|Hayden Blackman]] and published [[June 17]], [[2002]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />
